title: Sorbus thuringiaca
intro: Sorbus thuringiaca, also known as the Thuringian mountain ash, is a species of flowering plant in the rose family that is native to Central Europe. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, providing relief from various health issues such as fever, cough, and digestive problems. The bioactive constituents of Sorbus thuringiaca include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and anthocyanins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Sorbus thuringiaca include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, as well as to promote overall well-being.
taxonomy: Sorbus thuringiaca belongs to the family Rosaceae. It is a genus of about 100 species of deciduous trees and shrubs. The species Sorbus thuringiaca is native to parts of Europe. It is closely related to other species of mountain ash, which are often confused with one another. The Sorbus thuringiaca is sometimes referred to as the Thuringian whitebeam.
uses: Sorbus thuringiaca is known to have various medicinal uses, particularly in traditional medicine. Its bark and leaves are used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The plant's extracts are also used to treat skin conditions and wounds due to its antiseptic properties. In traditional medicine, Sorbus thuringiaca is used to treat respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds, due to its expectorant properties. The plant's leaves and bark are also used to treat menstrual cramps and other gynecological issues.

The bark and leaves of the plant are rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which are known for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.
